{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01CXWexr7ZiA9oc52K38P312","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DXTpZ1XGhEHowDx7UMwFka","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'EOF'\nimport os, re, glob\nfrom collections import Counter\n\nfiles = glob.glob('raw_service_reports/**/*.txt', recursive=True)\nprint(\"total files:\", len(files))\n\n# Parse all, collect keys and value distributions\nkeysets = Counter()\nworktype = Counter()\nreason = Counter()\nstatus = Counter()\nsample_keys = None\nmalformed = []\nfor fp in files:\n    with open(fp) as f:\n        txt = f.read()\n    fields = {}\n    for line in txt.splitlines():\n        line=line.strip()\n        if not line: continue\n        if ':' in line:\n            k,v = line.split(':',1)\n            # handle time values with colon\n            fields.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip())\n    keysets[tuple(sorted(fields.keys()))] += 1\n    worktype[fields.get('WORK TYPE')] += 1\n    reason[fields.get('REPORTED REASON')] += 1\n    status[fields.get('FINAL STATUS')] += 1\n\nprint(\"\\n=== keyset variants ===\")\nfor k,c in keysets.most_common():\n    print(c, k)\nprint(\"\\n=== WORK TYPE ===\")\nfor k,c in worktype.most_common(): print(c,k)\nprint(\"\\n=== REPORTED REASON ===\")\nfor k,c in reason.most_common(): print(c,k)\nprint(\"\\n=== FINAL STATUS ===\")\nfor k,c in status.most_common(): print(c,k)\nEOF","description":"Parse all service reports and tally field variety"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":546,"cache_read_input_tokens":26211,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":546,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":68,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cf311ab-fa40-46b1-8849-ddf82d3b8754","uuid":"f4c57a7b-351d-45f3-93d9-f11c80996ad4","request_id":"req_011CbfBUJJrPAyEWX4wEuDgz"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01PtpdjR429Axc6nY9KNjTNZ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0122K99KPUJgBQNrJfczAdGs","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, glob, re, os\n\n# Parse all raw service reports\nrows=[]\nfor fp in glob.glob('raw_service_reports/**/*.txt', recursive=True):\n    d={}\n    for line in open(fp):\n        line=line.strip()\n        if ':' in line:\n            k,v=line.split(':',1)\n            d[k.strip()]=v.strip()\n    rows.append(dict(\n        day=int(d['DAY']),\n        work_order_id=d['WORK ORDER'],\n        machine_id=d['MACHINE'],\n        technician_id=d['TECHNICIAN'],\n        work_type=d['WORK TYPE'],\n        reason=d['REPORTED REASON'],\n        started=d['STARTED'],\n        completed=d['COMPLETED'],\n        path=fp,\n    ))\nmw=pd.DataFrame(rows)\nprint(\"maintenance events:\", len(mw))\nprint(\"machines serviced:\", mw['machine_id'].nunique())\nprint(\"technicians:\", mw['technician_id'].nunique())\nprint(\"work orders unique:\", mw['work_order_id'].nunique())\n\n# cross check against csv\ncsv=pd.read_csv('maintenance_work_orders.csv')\nprint(\"\\ncsv WO == parsed WO:\", set(csv['work_order_id'])==set(mw['work_order_id']))\n\ndef to_min(t):\n    h,m=t.split(':'); return int(h)*60+int(m)\nmw['start_min']=mw['day']*1440+mw['started'].map(to_min)\nmw['comp_min']=mw['day']*1440+mw['completed'].map(to_min)\n# check completed >= started\nprint(\"completed>=started all:\", (mw['comp_min']>=mw['start_min']).all())\n\n# events per machine\nprint(\"\\nevents per machine describe:\")\nprint(mw.groupby('machine_id').size().describe())\n# work orders per technician\nprint(\"\\nWOs per technician describe:\")\nprint(mw.groupby('technician_id').size().describe())\n\nmw.to_pickle('/tmp/maint.pkl')\nprint(\"saved\")\nEOF","description":"Parse maintenance events into dataframe"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":791,"cache_read_input_tokens":31856,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":791,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":61,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cf311ab-fa40-46b1-8849-ddf82d3b8754","uuid":"1df82b20-254b-440a-b8f5-70e43d990dde","request_id":"req_011CbfBZ7fHPXFzbkvL1PrQR"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01RWfRCba4z4QNvWYnoc4bZp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NERkF9E5h7tmP5ABo5VVo9","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nb = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/batches.pkl')\nmw = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/maint.pkl')\n\n# Build per-machine event timeline sorted by start_min\nmw = mw.sort_values(['machine_id','start_min']).reset_index(drop=True)\n\n# For each machine, list of (start_min, comp_min, wo, tech, idx)\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nevents = defaultdict(list)\nfor r in mw.itertuples():\n    events[r.machine_id].append((r.start_min, r.comp_min, r.work_order_id, r.technician_id))\nfor m in events: events[m].sort()\n\nimport bisect\ndef attribute(machine, bstart):\n    evs = events.get(machine, [])\n    if not evs: return (None,None)\n    starts=[e[0] for e in evs]\n    # find events with comp_min < bstart; among those pick latest comp; ensure bstart < next start\n    # iterate: find the event whose window contains bstart\n    # window of event i: (comp_i, start_{i+1})\n    owner=None\n    for i,(s,c,wo,tech) in enumerate(evs):\n        nxt = evs[i+1][0] if i+1<len(evs) else float('inf')\n        if c < bstart < nxt:\n            owner=(wo,tech)\n            # don't break: later events could also satisfy? windows are disjoint by construction mostly\n    return owner if owner else (None,None)\n\nres = b.apply(lambda r: attribute(r['machine_id'], r['start_min']), axis=1)\nb['wo']=[x[0] for x in res]\nb['tech']=[x[1] for x in res]\nprint(\"batches attributed:\", b['wo'].notna().sum(), \"/\", len(b))\nprint(\"unattributed:\", b['wo'].isna().sum())\n# unattributed = produced before first maintenance, or during a maintenance window\nrej=b[b['rej']==1]\nprint(\"\\nrejected batches attributed:\", rej['wo'].notna().sum(), \"/\", len(rej))\nb.to_pickle('/tmp/batches_attr.pkl')\nEOF","description":"Attribute each batch to its owning maintenance interval"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3289,"cache_read_input_tokens":33992,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3289,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":40,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cf311ab-fa40-46b1-8849-ddf82d3b8754","uuid":"1e935ee1-7491-4d5d-a3dd-8494a181f166","request_id":"req_011CbfBd3yPwF1iAA6GmHm8g"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01R54tNTogvUrfV77hQx9ELe","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01VxdeyBKFGiETKPhcT34FDE","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nb=pd.read_pickle('/tmp/batches_resid.pkl')\nba=b[b['wo'].notna()].copy()\n\n# per-product robust sigma (MAD over ALL batches of product -> robust to sparse faults)\ndef mad(x): return 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(x-np.median(x)))\nsig_p=b.groupby('product_id')['resid'].apply(mad)\nba['sig_p']=ba['product_id'].map(sig_p)\n\ncell=ba.groupby(['machine_id','product_id','wo','tech']).agg(\n    n=('batch_id','count'), nrej=('rej','sum'),\n    mean_resid=('resid','mean'), min_resid=('resid','min'),\n    max_resid=('resid','max'), sig_p=('sig_p','first')).reset_index()\ncell['z']=cell['mean_resid']/(cell['sig_p']/np.sqrt(cell['n']))\n\ndef run(zthr, guard_max=0.0):\n    fc=cell[(cell['z']>zthr)&(cell['nrej']>0)&(cell['max_resid']>guard_max)].copy()\n    # rejected batches in fault cells\n    nrej=fc['nrej'].sum()\n    # confirm techs\n    tg=fc.groupby('tech').agg(nrej=('nrej','sum'), nwo=('wo','nunique')).reset_index()\n    conf=tg[(tg['nrej']>=3)&(tg['nwo']>=2)]['tech'].tolist()\n    # products per confirmed tech\n    ans={}\n    fcc=fc[fc['tech'].isin(conf)]\n    for prod,sub in fcc.groupby('product_id'):\n        techs=sorted(sub['tech'].unique())\n        ans[prod]=techs\n    return nrej, sorted(conf), ans, fc\n\nfor z in [4,5,6,8,10,13]:\n    nrej,conf,ans,fc=run(z)\n    print(f\"z>{z}: fault-cell rejected batches={nrej}, fault cells={len(fc)}, confirmed techs({len(conf)})={conf}\")\nprint()\n# detailed answer at z=6\nfor z in [5,6,8]:\n    nrej,conf,ans,fc=run(z)\n    print(f\"\\n===== ANSWER at z>{z} ({len(ans)} products) =====\")\n    import json\n    print(json.dumps({k:ans[k] for k in sorted(ans)}, indent=0).replace('\\n',' '))\nEOF","description":"Run full pipeline across z cutoffs"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4060,"cache_read_input_tokens":79616,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4060,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":49,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cf311ab-fa40-46b1-8849-ddf82d3b8754","uuid":"fd8dc3be-1ebd-43e4-b7d7-3a618159de94","request_id":"req_011CbfC9fsUq4EvprRqa2aXq"}

1.4826*np.median(np.abs(x-np.median(x)))\nsig_p=b.groupby('product_id')['resid'].apply(mad)\nba['sig_p']=ba['product_id'].map(sig_p)\ncell=ba.groupby(['machine_id','product_id','wo','tech']).agg(\n    n=('batch_id','count'), nrej=('rej','sum'), mean_resid=('resid','mean'),\n    min_resid=('resid','min'), max_resid=('resid','max'), sig_p=('sig_p','first')).reset_index()\ncell['z']=cell['mean_resid']/(cell['sig_p']/np.sqrt(cell['n']))\n\n# Product-specificity check: for clear multi-batch fault cells, are OTHER products in same wo normal?\nfc=cell[(cell['z']>6)&(cell['nrej']>0)]\nmulti=fc[fc['n']>=2].sort_values('z',ascending=False)\nprint(\"=== Product-specificity: for each clear multi-batch fault cell, max z of OTHER products in same WO ===\")\nbad=0\nfor r in multi.itertuples():\n    others=cell[(cell['wo']==r.wo)&(cell['product_id']!=r.product_id)]\n    omaxz=others['z'].max() if len(others) else np.nan\n    span=ba[(ba['wo']==r.wo)&(ba['product_id']==r.product_id)]['production_day']\n    da.span=span.max()-span.min()\n    flag = '' if (pd.isna(omaxz) or omaxz<4) else ' <-- other product also elevated!'\n    if flag: bad+=1\n    print(f\"{r.wo} {r.machine_id} {r.product_id} z={r.z:.1f} dayspan={span.max()-span.min()} | max z other products={omaxz:.2f}{flag}\")\nprint(\"cells where another product also elevated:\", bad)\n\n# 2 unattributed rejected batches\nuna=b[(b['rej']==1)&(b['wo'].isna())]\nprint(\"\\n=== 2 unattributed rejected batches (cannot link to any tech) 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(x-np.median(x)))\nsig_p=b.groupby('product_id')['resid'].apply(mad)\nba['sig_p']=ba['product_id'].map(sig_p)\ncell=ba.groupby(['machine_id','product_id','wo','tech']).agg(\n    n=('batch_id','count'), nrej=('rej','sum'), mean_resid=('resid','mean'),\n    max_resid=('resid','max'), sig_p=('sig_p','first')).reset_index()\ncell['z']=cell['mean_resid']/(cell['sig_p']/np.sqrt(cell['n']))\n\n# FAULT CELL = persistent elevation: cell-level z>6 AND contains >=1 rejected batch\nfc=cell[(cell['z']>6)&(cell['nrej']>0)].copy()\nprint(\"Total fault cells:\", len(fc), \"| fault-caused rejected batches:\", fc['nrej'].sum())\n\n# ALL techs owning fault cells\nallt=fc.groupby('tech').agg(nrej=('nrej','sum'), nwo=('wo','nunique'),\n                            prods=('product_id', lambda s: sorted(set(s)))).reset_index()\nallt=allt.sort_values('nrej',ascending=False)\nprint(\"\\n=== All techs with fault cells ===\")\nfor r in allt.itertuples():\n    conf = (r.nrej>=3 and r.nwo>=2)\n    print(f\"{r.tech}: rej={r.nrej} wo={r.nwo} {'CONFIRMED' if conf else 'excluded(isolated)'} prods={r.prods}\")\n\nCONF=allt[(allt['nrej']>=3)&(allt['nwo']>=2)]['tech'].tolist()\nfcc=fc[fc['tech'].isin(CONF)]\n\n# product -> sorted techs\nans={}\nfor prod,sub in fcc.groupby('product_id'):\n    ans[prod]=sorted(sub['tech'].unique())\nans={k:ans[k] for k in sorted(ans)}\nprint(\"\\n=== FINAL ANSWER ===\")\nprint(json.dumps(ans, indent=2))\nwith open('/app/answer.json','w') as f:\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_018MmYkNaxpH7hTjYZpLzzd5","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The analysis is complete. The answer has been written to `/app/answer.json`.\n\n## Summary\n\n**Approach:** I reconstructed the hidden causal chain through five stages:\n\n1. **Parsed the raw service reports** (`/app/raw_service_reports/`) to recover what the CSV omitted: each work order's machine, technician, and start/completion times (3,074 events across 56 machines, 42 technicians).\n\n2. **Built maintenance intervals & attribution.** Converting days+times to absolute timestamps, I assigned each batch to the maintenance event whose window `(completion_i, start_{i+1})` contained its production start — exactly the a/b/c linking rule. Crucially, I used *production* timing, not inspection day (inspections lag 0–10 days). 340 of 342 rejected batches were attributable; the 2 unattributable ones occurred before their machine's first maintenance.\n\n3. **Modeled the confounders to rule them out.** Naive attribution implicated nearly every technician because rejections scale with batch volume. So I built a baseline: a robust per-(machine, product) median plus a regression on the explicitly-named confounders (factory load/humidity/material/power indices, job difficulty, operator experience). The residual isolates unexplained elevation. **Natural variation has a hard ceiling** — of 43,708 non-rejected batches, *zero* exceed residual 0.04, while faults reach 0.05–0.20.\n\n4. **Detected faults via persistence.** A hidden fault persists across the whole interval and affects only specific products. I tested each (machine, product, interval) cell for *systematic, persistent* elevation (z-test on mean residual). This correctly distinguishes faults (all batches of one product elevated for the whole interval, other products untouched — verified directly) from non-persistent natural spikes and factory-wide bad days. Two independent criteria (cell z>6 and the absolute natural-ceiling rule) agreed on the same 8 technicians, and the result is stable across cutoffs.\n\n5. **Confirmed technicians & reported products** per the thresholds (≥3 fault-linked rejected batches across ≥2 work orders), excluding isolated cases like TECH_010 (5 rejections but a single work order).\n\n**8 confirmed technicians**, mapped to the products their faulty maintenance caused:\n\n```json\n{\"P_001\":[\"TECH_014\",\"TECH_027\",\"TECH_031\"],\"P_004\":[\"TECH_003\",\"TECH_030\"],\"P_006\":[\"TECH_002\"],\"P_008\":[\"TECH_031\"],\"P_015\":[\"TECH_014\",\"TECH_021\"],\"P_018\":[\"TECH_014\"],\"P_019\":[\"TECH_030\"],\"P_024\":[\"TECH_003\",\"TECH_014\",\"TECH_027\",\"TECH_037\"],\"P_026\":[\"TECH_014\",\"TECH_031\"],\"P_027\":[\"TECH_002\",\"TECH_021\"],\"P_029\":[\"TECH_002\",\"TECH_003\",\"TECH_014\",\"TECH_027\",\"TECH_037\"]}\n```"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":874,"cache_read_input_tokens":132182,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":874,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cf311ab-fa40-46b1-8849-ddf82d3b8754","uuid":"6b84a582-5cdd-47e2-9ff3-66f152e9a6ea","request_id":"req_011CbfCgEBWTLRA4vzUjgQHF"}
